statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{x-2}+\frac{4}{x-2}}}{{3-\frac{1}{x+2}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{x-2}+\frac{4}{x-2}} and {3-\frac{1}{x+2}}, which is {\left(x+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-2\right)}.
Since x+2 was canceled, x \ne -2 is a lost domain condition.
Although x-2 was also canceled, x \ne 2 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
